records.ety: [L., one who sits beside, the assistant of a judge, fr. assidēre. See Assession. LL., one who arranges of determines the taxes, fr. assidēre. See Assess, v., and cf. Cessor.]

defs: One appointed or elected to assist a judge or magistrate with his special knowledge of the subject to be decided; as legal assessors, nautical assessors.
One who sits by another, as next in dignity, or as an assistant and adviser; an associate in office.
quotes: Whence to his Son, The assessor of his throne, he thus began.
With his ignorance, his inclinations, and his fancy, as his assessors in judgment.

defs: One appointed to assess persons or property for the purpose of taxation.
